title: A1 Review & Test
description: Review everything from Units 1-7! A full day scenario: hotel, restaurant, shopping, daily routine, and meeting people.
descriptionTr: Ünite 1-7'deki her şeyi tekrarla! Tam bir gün senaryosu: otel, restoran, alışveriş, günlük rutin ve insanlarla tanışma.

vocabulary.example: Excuse me, where is the nearest restaurant?
vocabulary.extra: Excuse me, can you help me with my luggage?
vocabulary.extraTr: Affedersiniz, bavulumla bana yardım edebilir misiniz?

vocabulary.example: Pleased to meet you! I'm Eyüp.
vocabulary.extra: I'm very pleased with the hotel room.

vocabulary.example: It's a quarter past nine. The taxi is here.
vocabulary.extra: I'll meet you in a quarter of an hour.

vocabulary.example: The train leaves at half past ten.
vocabulary.extra: I waited for half an hour at the restaurant.

vocabulary.example: I'm exhausted after walking all day in the city.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bütün gün şehirde yürüdükten sonra bitkinim.
vocabulary.extra: She looks exhausted. She needs to rest.

vocabulary.example: I feel more confident when I speak English now.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Artık İngilizce konuşurken kendimi daha özgüvenli hissediyorum.
vocabulary.extra: She is confident about the job interview.

vocabulary.example: Can you recommend a good restaurant near here?
vocabulary.exampleTr: Buralarda iyi bir restoran tavsiye edebilir misiniz?
vocabulary.extra: I recommend the chicken soup. It's delicious!
vocabulary.extraTr: Tavuk çorbasını tavsiye ederim. Çok lezzetli!

vocabulary.example: I always compare prices before I buy something.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bir şey almadan önce her zaman fiyatları karşılaştırırım.
vocabulary.extra: Don't compare yourself with other people.

vocabulary.example: Whose bag is this? Is it yours?
vocabulary.extra: Whose turn is it to pay?

vocabulary.example: I commute to work by bus every day.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Her gün otobüsle işe gidip geliyorum.
vocabulary.extra: My commute takes about 45 minutes.
vocabulary.extraTr: İşe gidiş geliş yaklaşık 45 dakika sürüyor.

vocabulary.example: The deadline is tomorrow. I need to finish this today.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Son tarih yarın. Bunu bugün bitirmem lazım.
vocabulary.extra: We have a tight deadline for this project.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu proje için sıkı bir teslim tarihimiz var.

vocabulary.example: Would you like an appetizer before your main course?
vocabulary.exampleTr: Ana yemekten önce bir başlangıç ister misiniz?
vocabulary.extra: The soup is the best appetizer on the menu.

vocabulary.example: This food is absolutely delicious!
vocabulary.extra: The breakfast at this hotel is delicious.

vocabulary.example: Can I have the receipt, please?
vocabulary.extra: Keep the receipt. You might need it later.

vocabulary.example: Is there a discount for students?
vocabulary.extra: Everything is 20% discount this week.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu hafta her şey yüzde 20 indirimli.

vocabulary.example: I'd like to check out, please. Room 305.
vocabulary.extra: What time is check out?

vocabulary.example: I have a reservation for two people.
vocabulary.extra: Do I need a reservation for tonight?
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu akşam için rezervasyon yapmam lazım mı?

vocabulary.example: Is this table available?
vocabulary.extra: Sorry, there are no rooms available tonight.
vocabulary.extraTr: Üzgünüz, bu gece müsait oda yok.

vocabulary.example: Here's your change — five dollars.
vocabulary.extra: Can I change my room? This one is too noisy.
vocabulary.extraTr: Odamı değiştirebilir miyim? Bu çok gürültülü.

vocabulary.example: Are you ready to order?
vocabulary.extra: I'd like to order the grilled chicken, please.
vocabulary.extraTr: Izgara tavuk sipariş etmek istiyorum, lütfen.

vocabulary.example: Can I have the bill, please?
vocabulary.extra: The total bill is 85 dollars.

vocabulary.example: In America, you usually leave a 15-20% tip.
vocabulary.extra: Here's a tip: always carry some cash with you.
vocabulary.extraTr: İşte bir ipucu: yanında her zaman biraz nakit bulundur.

vocabulary.example: What size do you wear? Medium or large?
vocabulary.exampleTr: Hangi bedeni giyiyorsun? Orta mı büyük mü?
vocabulary.extra: Do you have this in a smaller size?
vocabulary.extraTr: Bunun daha küçük bedeni var mı?

vocabulary.example: Can I try on this jacket?
vocabulary.extra: You should try on the shoes before you buy them.

vocabulary.example: I don't want to spend too much money today.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bugün çok fazla para harcamak istemiyorum.
vocabulary.extra: How much time do you spend learning English?
vocabulary.extraTr: İngilizce öğrenmeye ne kadar zaman harcıyorsun?

vocabulary.example: Is breakfast included in the hotel price?
vocabulary.extra: I usually have eggs and toast for breakfast.
vocabulary.extraTr: Kahvaltıda genellikle yumurta ve tost yerim.

vocabulary.example: What time does the bus arrive?
vocabulary.extra: We arrive in New York at 3 PM.

vocabulary.example: I need to leave the hotel before 11 AM.
vocabulary.extra: What time do you leave for work?

vocabulary.example: Can I return this shirt? It doesn't fit.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu gömleği iade edebilir miyim? Olmuyor.
vocabulary.extra: I return home at 6 PM every day.

vocabulary.example: What is the address of the restaurant?
vocabulary.extra: Can you write down the address for me?

grammar.title: A1 Grammar Review
grammar.explanation: This is a complete review of all A1 grammar topics from Units 1-7. We covered: Be verb (am/is/are), Present Simple with do/does, WH Questions, Would like / Can I have, Time expressions, and Frequency adverbs. Master these and you are ready for A2!
grammar.explanationTr: Bu, Ünite 1-7'deki tüm A1 gramer konularının tam bir tekrarıdır. Gördüklerimiz: Be fiili (am/is/are), Geniş Zaman do/does ile, WH Soruları, Would like / Can I have, Zaman ifadeleri ve Sıklık zarfları. Bunları öğren ve A2'ye hazırsın!
table: Be Verb: I am happy. She is tired.
I am not tired. She isn't hungry.
Are you ready? Is he a doctor?
am → I / is → he,she,it / are → you,we,they
Present Simple: I eat breakfast at 8.
I don't eat meat. She doesn't like fish.
Do you like coffee? Does he work here?
He/She/It → fiil + s/es. Soru ve olumsuzda -s düşer!
WH Questions: Where is the hotel?
What do you want? How much does it cost?
Who is your teacher? Whose bag is this?
WH + be/do/does + özne + fiil
Would like: I'd like a coffee, please.
I wouldn't like that. No, thank you.
Would you like some water?
Would like = want'ın kibar hali. Restoranda kullan!
Can I have: Can I have the bill?
I can't have sugar. (izin/yetenek)
Can I try this on? Can you help me?
Can + fiil (yalın hali). Çok kullanışlı!
Time: at 9 o'clock, on Monday, in January
It's quarter past ten. It's half past two.
What time is it? When is the meeting?
at → saat / on → gün / in → ay, yıl, mevsim
Frequency: I always eat breakfast.
She never drinks coffee.
Do you usually walk to work?
always > usually > often > sometimes > rarely > never → fiilden ÖNCE

tips.en: He likes tea. Does he like tea? He doesn't like tea. — The -s moves to does/doesn't!
tips.tr: He likes tea. Does he like tea? He doesn't like tea. — Soru ve olumsuzda -s, does/doesn't'a geçer!

tips.en: I'm tired ≠ I'm tiring. Tired = how YOU feel. Tiring = the thing that makes you tired.
tips.tr: I'm tired ≠ I'm tiring. Tired = SENİN hissin. Tiring = seni yoran ŞEY.

tips.en: Would you like...? = Do you want...? (but more polite!). Use 'would like' in restaurants.
tips.tr: Would you like...? = Do you want...? (ama daha kibar!). Restoranlarda 'would like' kullan.

tips.en: There is + tekil: There is a hotel. There are + çoğul: There are two restaurants.
tips.tr: There is + tekil: There is a hotel. There are + çoğul: There are two restaurants.

tips.en: How much + sayılamayan: How much water? How many + sayılabilen: How many eggs?
tips.tr: How much + sayılamayan: How much water? How many + sayılabilen: How many eggs?

tips.en: In the morning / In the afternoon / In the evening — BUT: At night (not 'in the night'!)
tips.tr: In the morning / In the afternoon / In the evening — AMA: At night ('in the night' DEĞİL!)

reading.text:
My First Week in America

Hello! My name is Eyüp and I'm from Turkey. Last month, I traveled to New York for the first time. Let me tell you about my amazing first week!

On Monday, I arrived at the airport at 7 AM. I was very tired after the long flight. I took a taxi to my hotel in Manhattan. The taxi driver was friendly. He asked, "Is this your first time in New York?" I said, "Yes, it is!" He recommended a good restaurant near the hotel.

I checked into the hotel at 10 AM. The receptionist was very helpful. She said, "Your room is on the 5th floor. Breakfast is included and it's from 7 to 10 AM." I went to my room, took a shower, and rested for two hours.

In the afternoon, I was hungry, so I walked to the restaurant the taxi driver recommended. It was a small Italian place. The waiter asked, "Would you like to see the menu?" I ordered a chicken pasta and a glass of lemonade. The food was absolutely delicious! The bill was 22 dollars plus tip.

On Tuesday, I went shopping at a big supermarket. I needed water, fruit, and some snacks for my room. Everything was more expensive than Turkey! A bottle of water cost 2 dollars. I also bought a souvenir t-shirt from a small store. It had "I Love NY" on it. The cashier gave me a 10% discount because I paid cash.

Every morning, I had breakfast at the hotel. I usually ate eggs, toast, and fresh orange juice. The coffee was strong and delicious. After breakfast, I always walked around the city. I visited Times Square, Central Park, and the Statue of Liberty.

On Friday, I met an American man named Jake at a café. He asked, "Where are you from?" I said, "I'm from Turkey." He was very interested in Turkish culture. We talked for two hours! He recommended some places to visit. Jake said, "You should try the pizza in Brooklyn. It's the best in New York!" We exchanged phone numbers and he said, "See you next time you're in New York!"

My first week in America was incredible. I practiced my English every day, tried new food, met friendly people, and learned so much. I can't wait to go back!
reading.textTr:
Amerika'daki İlk Haftam

Merhaba! Benim adım Eyüp ve Türkiye'denim. Geçen ay ilk kez New York'a seyahat ettim. Size harika ilk haftamı anlatayım!

Pazartesi sabah 7'de havalimanına vardım. Uzun uçuştan sonra çok yorgundum. Manhattan'daki otelime taksi tuttum. Taksi şoförü samimiydi. "New York'a ilk gelişiniz mi?" diye sordu. "Evet, öyle!" dedim. Otelin yakınında iyi bir restoran önerdi.

Otele sabah 10'da giriş yaptım. Resepsiyonist çok yardımseverdi. "Odanız 5. katta. Kahvaltı dahil ve 7'den 10'a kadar" dedi. Odama gittim, duş aldım ve iki saat dinlendim.

Öğleden sonra acıktım, bu yüzden taksi şoförünün önerdiği restorana yürüdüm. Küçük bir İtalyan restoranıydı. Garson, "Menüyü görmek ister misiniz?" dedi. Tavuklu makarna ve bir bardak limonata sipariş ettim. Yemek kesinlikle nefisti! Hesap bahşiş hariç 22 dolardı.

Salı günü büyük bir süpermarkete alışverişe gittim. Odam için su, meyve ve biraz atıştırmalık lazımdı. Her şey Türkiye'den daha pahalıydı! Bir şişe su 2 dolardı. Ayrıca küçük bir mağazadan hediyelik tişört aldım. Üzerinde "I Love NY" yazıyordu. Kasiyer nakit ödediğim için yüzde 10 indirim yaptı.

Her sabah otelde kahvaltı yaptım. Genelde yumurta, tost ve taze portakal suyu yedim. Kahve güçlü ve lezzetliydi. Kahvaltıdan sonra her zaman şehirde yürüyüşe çıktım. Times Square, Central Park ve Özgürlük Heykeli'ni ziyaret ettim.

Cuma günü bir kafede Jake adında bir Amerikalıyla tanıştım. "Nerelisiniz?" dedi. "Türkiye'denim" dedim. Türk kültürüne çok ilgiliydi. İki saat konuştuk! Gezilecek yerler önerdi. Jake, "Brooklyn'de pizza denemelisiniz. New York'un en iyisi!" dedi. Telefon numaralarımızı değiştirdik ve "Bir dahaki sefere New York'a geldiğinizde görüşürüz!" dedi.

Amerika'daki ilk haftam inanılmazdı. Her gün İngilizcemi pratik ettim, yeni yemekler denedim, samimi insanlarla tanıştım ve çok şey öğrendim. Geri dönmek için sabırsızlanıyorum!

questions.question: How did Eyüp get from the airport to the hotel?
What did Eyüp order at the Italian restaurant?
questions.questionTr: Eyüp İtalyan restoranında ne sipariş etti?

questions.question: Why did the cashier give Eyüp a discount?
What did Eyüp usually eat for breakfast?
What did Jake recommend to Eyüp?

fillBlanks.sentence: I ___ from Turkey. Nice to meet you!
She ___ a doctor at a big hospital.
He ___ like shopping. He prefers to stay home.
She ___ breakfast at 7 o'clock every morning.
I'd ___ a glass of water, please.
I ___ go to the gym. Maybe once a year.
Can I have the ___, please? I want to pay.
I need to ___ out before 11 AM.

multipleChoice.question: "I ___ tired after the long flight." Boşluğa ne gelir?
"___ she work on Saturdays?" Boşluğa ne gelir?
"Would you like some coffee?" ne demek?
"I ___ eat meat. I'm vegetarian." Boşluğa ne gelir?
"He go___ to work at 9 AM." Doğru ek hangisi?
"She ___ drinks coffee. She hates it." Hangi sıklık zarfı gelir?
"Can I have the receipt?" ne demek?

sentenceOrder.correct: I'd like to check out please
Can I have the bill please
She always eats breakfast at eight
Is there a discount for students
I don't usually spend much money
What time does the bus arrive
Can I try on this jacket

translation.english: I'd like to check out, please.
acceptAlso: I would like to check out please
I want to check out please

translation.turkish: Buralarda iyi bir restoran tavsiye edebilir misiniz?
translation.english: Can you recommend a good restaurant near here?
acceptAlso: Can you recommend a good restaurant nearby

translation.english: Can I have the bill, please?
acceptAlso: Can I have the bill please
Can I get the bill please

translation.english: I eat breakfast at 7 o'clock every morning.
acceptAlso: I have breakfast at 7 every morning
Every morning I eat breakfast at 7

translation.english: Is there a discount for students?
acceptAlso: Do you have a discount for students

dialog.title: Eyüp's Full Day in New York
dialog.context: Eyüp New York'ta bir gününü geçiriyor. Otelden çıkış yapıyor, taksi tutuyor, restoranda öğle yemeği yiyor, markete gidiyor ve bir arkadaşıyla buluşuyor.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Good morning! I'd like to check out, please. Room 305.
lines.textTr: Günaydın! Çıkış yapmak istiyorum, lütfen. Oda 305.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good morning, sir! Did you enjoy your stay?
lines.textTr: Günaydın, efendim! Kalışınızdan memnun kaldınız mı?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yes, it was great! The breakfast was delicious. How much is the total?
lines.textTr: Evet, harikaydı! Kahvaltı çok lezzetliydi. Toplam ne kadar?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Your total is 450 dollars for three nights. Would you like a receipt?
lines.textTr: Toplamınız üç gece için 450 dolar. Fiş ister misiniz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yes, please. Can you call a taxi for me?
lines.textTr: Evet, lütfen. Benim için bir taksi çağırabilir misiniz?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Where to, buddy?
lines.textTr: Nereye, dostum?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Can you take me to Mario's Italian Restaurant on 5th Avenue, please?
lines.textTr: Beni 5. Cadde'deki Mario'nun İtalyan Restoranına götürebilir misiniz, lütfen?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Sure thing! That's about 15 minutes from here. Good choice — their food is amazing!
lines.textTr: Tabii! Buradan yaklaşık 15 dakika. İyi seçim — yemekleri harika!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Welcome to Mario's! Table for one? Are you ready to order?
lines.textTr: Mario's'a hoş geldiniz! Bir kişilik masa? Sipariş vermeye hazır mısınız?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Can I see the menu first? And I'd like a glass of water, please.
lines.textTr: Önce menüyü görebilir miyim? Ve bir bardak su istiyorum, lütfen.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Of course! Would you like an appetizer while you look at the menu?
lines.textTr: Tabii! Menüye bakarken bir başlangıç ister misiniz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: What do you recommend?
lines.textTr: Ne tavsiye edersiniz?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: The tomato soup is very popular. For the main course, I recommend the grilled chicken.
lines.textTr: Domates çorbası çok popüler. Ana yemek için ızgara tavuğu tavsiye ederim.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I'll have the soup and the grilled chicken, please. Can I have the bill after?
lines.textTr: Çorbayı ve ızgara tavuğu alayım, lütfen. Sonra hesabı alabilir miyim?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Excuse me, how much is the total?
lines.textTr: Affedersiniz, toplam ne kadar?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That's 28 dollars, sir. Cash or card?
lines.textTr: 28 dolar, efendim. Nakit mi kart mı?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Card, please. And here's a 5 dollar tip. The food was delicious!
lines.textTr: Kartla, lütfen. Ve 5 dolar bahşiş. Yemek çok lezzetliydi!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hey, Eyüp! Over here! How are you doing, man?
lines.textTr: Hey, Eyüp! Burada! Nasılsın, dostum?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Jake! Great to see you! I'm good but a little exhausted. I walked a lot today.
lines.textTr: Jake! Seni görmek harika! İyiyim ama biraz bitkinim. Bugün çok yürüdüm.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Would you like some coffee? My treat! So, do you like New York?
lines.textTr: Kahve ister misin? Ben ısmarlıyorum! Peki, New York'u seviyor musun?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I love it! The food is amazing, people are friendly, and the city never sleeps. I always learn something new here.
lines.textTr: Bayılıyorum! Yemekler harika, insanlar samimi ve şehir hiç uyumuyor. Burada her zaman yeni bir şey öğreniyorum.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Your English is much better now! I'm really pleased to hear that. See you next time, buddy!
lines.textTr: İngilizcen çok daha iyi artık! Bunu duymak beni gerçekten memnun etti. Bir dahaki sefere görüşürüz, dostum!

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thanks, Jake! See you! And thank you for everything. Goodbye!
lines.textTr: Teşekkürler, Jake! Görüşürüz! Ve her şey için teşekkürler. Hoşça kal!

questions.question: How much did Eyüp pay for the hotel?
questions.questionTr: Eyüp otel için ne kadar ödedi?

questions.question: What did Eyüp order at the restaurant?
How much tip did Eyüp leave?
What does Jake think about Eyüp's English?
questions.questionTr: Jake, Eyüp'ün İngilizcesi hakkında ne düşünüyor?
